item.title: Great SCP
item.content:encoded: Though this SCP was from last series, I do believe that it is greatly underrated and deserves a lot more attention. Author, keep up the impressive work, I do respect you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The character you paint here is thoroughly enchanting. I'm surprised this isn't more highly rated! +1

However, I have a quibble with the final paragraph, which seems to imply that the Class-D was freely roaming his hometown, rather than in Foundation custody. I think the swan story would have been a fine ending, especially since it resolves the arc in the relationship between 4448 and the Foundation.

item.title: Re: Nice!
item.content:encoded: I'd love to do some cross-linking with this SCP, as I think the interactions could get quite interesting.

item.title: Nice!
item.content:encoded: Definite +1 for me. Potential for cross-testing with other SCP's too, and I find myself wondering what would happen if you tried to give it two, or if you presented two trinkets which one it would prefer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am REALLY surprised by how much I enjoyed this character. Very awesome stuff! 1+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My mistake, I goofed and completely forgot a word. It's been fixed now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm curious what effect being heated to 50°C above the boiling point of water has on the liquid in the lake.

Other than that, I enjoyed this. It's a weird crazy lake monster that seems almost programmed to perform a certain function, and becomes truculent when prevented from doing so. There's a definite folklore/mythic quality to that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
that Personnel D-2449 had strong connections to.

Um. Shouldn't this be the close friend's gang and not the D-2449's gang?

Given the gang would want to do a revenge killing, I'd wager, on D-2449 after their member died. Unless I read this wrong.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Simple and I like it. I got a kick out of the twists in the parables. I think it was a good structural move to separate the substantial responses from the entity by etcetera outcomes because I was aching to read another parable and was pulled along the article like a reeled-in fish.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the criticism, glad you enjoyed the article!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In terms of overall narrative, this doesn't feel like it particularly goes anywhere, but I found your character to be pretty charming, I think he's quite memorable in terms of construction (appearence, mannerisms, ability). +1
